text.title: AI Code-Review Impact Suggestion Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the ai-cr-impact-suggestion methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale. The terminal `skip-this-methodology` rule is the safety valve invoked by the decision tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: Analysis MUST be sourced from a real module graph; AST-only or diff-only inputs disallowed.
Each touched module MUST be flagged with coverage delta + uncovered branches.
rule.rationale: Hidden coverage gaps cause production incidents.

rule.statement: Files with churn > p90 in trailing 90d MUST be marked `hot-spot: true`.
Comment MUST start with `ai_drafted: true` + model_id line.
rule.rationale: Unflagged AI comments erode reviewer trust.

rule.statement: Comment is a suggestion only; named reviewer MUST sign off before merge.
rule.rationale: Auto-merge on AI suggestion produces low-quality reviews.

rule.statement: Author MUST stop and route elsewhere when the decision-tree resolves to a skip leaf; partial application of this methodology against an unfit input is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Forcing the methodology onto a mismatched input produces low-quality artefacts and erodes trust in the corpus.
